title: Speaking with eloquence
setting: At Roberto's wedding, you're unexpectedly asked to say a few words. The skill: rise to the occasion with warmth and eloquence.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Maje, decí unas palabras. Sos parte de la familia.
steps.en: Dude, say a few words. You're part of the family.

options.es: Será un honor. Denme un momento para hacerle justicia.
options.en: It'll be an honor. Give me a moment to do it justice.
options.feedback: Gracious and self-assured — buying a beat to speak well rather than blurting. Poised.

options.es: No, no, mejor que hable otro.
options.en: No, no, better someone else speaks.
options.feedback: Refusing the honor at a friend's wedding stings. You have the words now — rise to it.
Warm and willing. A simple, gracious yes that honors the invitation.

options.es: Hace años llegué a esta tierra sin conocer a nadie.
options.en: Years ago I came to this land knowing no one.
options.feedback: Opening with your own story draws everyone in — a storyteller's instinct. Beautiful start.

options.es: Eh... pues... felicidades a los novios.
options.en: Uh... well... congrats to the couple.
options.feedback: Nerves are natural, but you can do better than filler. Take a breath and begin with heart.

options.es: Voy a ser breve porque hay comida esperando.
options.en: I'll be brief because there's food waiting.
options.feedback: A light joke can work, but at a wedding, lead with warmth before humor.

options.es: Roberto me dio su amistad, y con ella, un hogar.
options.en: Roberto gave me his friendship, and with it, a home.
options.feedback: A moving, well-built line — friendship to home is a gorgeous progression. The room is yours.

options.es: Son buenas personas y los quiero mucho.
options.en: They're good people and I love them a lot.
options.feedback: Sincere, but plain for a toast. This is the moment to let your eloquence shine.

options.es: Que vivan los novios y que sean felices.
options.en: Long live the couple, may they be happy.
options.feedback: A classic, heartfelt toast line — simple and traditional, lands warmly.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: (con lágrimas) Gracias, hermano. Sabía que dirías algo así.
steps.en: (tearfully) Thank you, brother. I knew you'd say something like that.

options.es: ¡Por los novios! Que su amor dure más que mis errores de español.
options.en: To the couple! May their love outlast my Spanish mistakes.
options.feedback: A toast that's heartfelt AND funny, with a humble callback to your journey. Absolutely perfect.

options.es: ¡Salud por una vida juntos llena de amor!
options.en: Cheers to a life together full of love!
options.feedback: A warm, classic toast that raises the glass beautifully. The room will cheer.
Undercutting a tender moment for a joke about food deflates it. Let the warmth crest first.
